name: Mathematics
description: Test your skills across arithmetic, algebra, geometry, and more!

questions.text: What is 15 × 12?
What is the square root of 144?
questions.explanation: 12 × 12 = 144, so the square root of 144 is 12.

questions.text: What is 3/4 + 1/4?
What is the value of π (pi) rounded to two decimal places?
questions.explanation: Pi (π) is approximately 3.14159..., which rounds to 3.14.

questions.text: How many sides does a hexagon have?
questions.explanation: A hexagon is a polygon with exactly six sides.

questions.text: What is 2⁸?
What is the perimeter of a square with side length 7?
questions.explanation: The perimeter of a square is 4 × side = 4 × 7 = 28.

questions.text: Which of the following is a prime number?
questions.explanation: 17 is prime because it has no divisors other than 1 and itself.

questions.text: What is 25% of 200?
What is the sum of angles in a triangle?
questions.explanation: The interior angles of any triangle always sum to 180°.

questions.text: What is 7! (7 factorial)?
What is 0.75 expressed as a fraction in lowest terms?
questions.explanation: 0.75 = 75/100 = 3/4 in lowest terms.

questions.text: What is the area of a rectangle with length 9 and width 4?
questions.explanation: Area of rectangle = length × width = 9 × 4 = 36.

questions.text: What is the next number in the Fibonacci sequence: 1, 1, 2, 3, 5, 8, ___?
questions.explanation: Each Fibonacci number is the sum of the two before it: 5 + 8 = 13.

questions.text: What is the least common multiple (LCM) of 4 and 6?
questions.explanation: The LCM of 4 and 6 is 12, the smallest number divisible by both.

questions.text: What is the Roman numeral for 49?
questions.explanation: 49 in Roman numerals is XLIX (XL = 40, IX = 9).

questions.text: Which mathematician is credited with developing calculus independently of Newton?
questions.explanation: Gottfried Wilhelm Leibniz developed calculus independently and simultaneously with Newton.

questions.text: If x + 5 = 13, what is x?
questions.explanation: Subtracting 5 from both sides: x = 13 - 5 = 8.

questions.text: What is the slope of the line passing through (2, 3) and (4, 7)?
What is the area of a circle with radius 5? (Use π ≈ 3.14)
What is the greatest common divisor (GCD) of 48 and 36?
questions.explanation: The GCD of 48 and 36 is 12, the largest number that divides both evenly.

questions.text: Solve: 3x - 7 = 2x + 5
What is the hypotenuse of a right triangle with legs 6 and 8?
questions.explanation: By the Pythagorean theorem: √(6² + 8²) = √(36 + 64) = √100 = 10.

questions.text: What is 2/3 ÷ 4/9?
questions.explanation: Dividing fractions: 2/3 × 9/4 = 18/12 = 3/2.

questions.text: What is the sum of the first 10 positive integers?
questions.explanation: Using the formula n(n+1)/2: 10 × 11 / 2 = 55.

questions.text: In which century did Leonhard Euler live?
questions.explanation: Euler was born in 1707 and died in 1783, living through the 18th century.

questions.text: What is the value of log₁₀(1000)?
questions.explanation: log₁₀(1000) = 3 because 10³ = 1000.

questions.text: What is the next term in the sequence: 2, 6, 18, 54, ___?
questions.explanation: Each term is multiplied by 3: 54 × 3 = 162.

questions.text: What is the interior angle of a regular pentagon?
questions.explanation: Interior angle of regular polygon = (n-2) × 180/n = 3 × 180/5 = 108°.

questions.text: Which theorem states that in a right triangle, a² + b² = c²?
questions.explanation: The Pythagorean Theorem relates the three sides of a right triangle.

questions.text: What is the probability of rolling a sum of 7 with two dice?
questions.explanation: There are 6 ways to roll a sum of 7 out of 36 total outcomes, giving 6/36 = 1/6.

questions.text: Evaluate: 4² + 3³
If a triangle has sides of length 5, 5, and 8, what type of triangle is it?
questions.explanation: An isosceles triangle has exactly two equal sides; here, two sides are both 5.

questions.text: What is 5/8 expressed as a percentage?
What is the formula for the volume of a cylinder?
questions.explanation: The volume of a cylinder is π × radius² × height = πr²h.

questions.text: What did Carl Friedrich Gauss famously calculate as a child?
options: The value of π to 100 digits
The prime factorization of large numbers
questions.explanation: Gauss reportedly summed integers from 1 to 100 almost instantly as a child.

questions.text: What is the derivative of x³?
questions.explanation: By the power rule, d/dx(xⁿ) = nxⁿ⁻¹, so d/dx(x³) = 3x².

questions.text: What is the sum of the infinite geometric series 1 + 1/2 + 1/4 + 1/8 + ...?
How many prime numbers are there between 1 and 50?
What is the determinant of the matrix [[3, 1], [2, 4]]?
Fermat's Last Theorem states that xⁿ + yⁿ = zⁿ has no positive integer solutions for n > 2. Who finally proved it in 1994?
questions.explanation: Andrew Wiles proved Fermat's Last Theorem in 1994 after working on it for seven years.

questions.text: What is ∫x² dx?
questions.explanation: By the power rule of integration, ∫xⁿ dx = xⁿ⁺¹/(n+1) + C = x³/3 + C.

questions.text: In modular arithmetic, what is 17 mod 5?
questions.explanation: 17 ÷ 5 = 3 remainder 2, so 17 mod 5 = 2.

questions.text: What is the number of ways to arrange 5 distinct objects in a line?
questions.explanation: The number of permutations of 5 distinct objects is 5! = 120.

questions.text: What is the value of e (Euler's number) to 2 decimal places?
questions.explanation: Euler's number e ≈ 2.71828..., which rounds to 2.71.

questions.text: What is the solution to x² - 5x + 6 = 0?
questions.explanation: Factoring: (x - 2)(x - 3) = 0, giving x = 2 or x = 3.

questions.text: What is the Goldbach conjecture?
options: Every odd number > 5 is the sum of three primes
Every even integer > 2 is the sum of two prime numbers
There are infinitely many twin primes
Every natural number is the product of primes
questions.explanation: Goldbach's conjecture, proposed in 1742, states every even integer greater than 2 is the sum of two primes.

questions.text: What is the binary representation of the decimal number 25?
Which number is both a perfect square and a perfect cube between 1 and 100?
questions.explanation: 64 = 8² = 4³, making it both a perfect square and a perfect cube.

questions.text: In graph theory, what is the maximum number of edges in a simple graph with 5 vertices?
questions.explanation: A complete graph K₅ has n(n-1)/2 = 5×4/2 = 10 edges.
